pullRequest.title: Fix issue 510 apriori multiprocessing
pullRequest.body:
Hi @rasbt,

I hope you're doing well! I’ve been working on a fix for issue #510, which has been open for a while regarding the lack of multiprocessing in the `apriori` algorithm.

In this PR, I’ve implemented `n_jobs` support specifically for dense DataFrames. The core of the change involves:
- Using `joblib.Parallel` and `delayed` to distribute the support-counting workload across CPU cores.
- Keeping the logic clean and ensuring that everything still works perfectly on a single core by default (`n_jobs=1`).
- Breaking down the combinations into chunks to keep the overhead low and the memory usage stable.

I’ve run the existing `pytest` suite and everything is passing (24 passed). I also did some local benchmarking—while there's a small overhead for tiny datasets, the speedup becomes quite noticeable once the itemset generation gets heavy.

Looking forward to your feedback!

Fixes #510

pullRequest.title: style: apply ruff formatting and fix label mapping for #589
pullRequest.body: Hi @rasbt, I hope you are doing well. I have submitted this PR to address issue #589, where plot_decision_regions was missing colors when dealing with non-consecutive or negative class labels (e.g., [-1, 1]). The Problem: The function previously relied on the actual class label values as indices for color mapping, which caused issues when labels were not starting from 0 or contained negative integers. The Solution: I modified the internal logic to map arbitrary class labels to a zero-indexed range (0, 1, 2...) specifically for the coloring and contouring process. This ensures that: Decision regions are always colored correctly regardless of the label values. The original labels are preserved for the scatter plot and legend to maintain data integrity. Changes made: Introduced a label_to_index mapping. Mapped Z predictions to Z_indexed for the contourf and contour calls. Verified the fix with a Perceptron classifier using [-1, 1] labels. Applied Ruff for formatting and linting to ensure code quality. Fixes #589

pullRequest.title: Docs: Update example 10 with custom Iris class names (#737)
pullRequest.body:
### Code of Conduct

I have reviewed the [Code of Conduct](https://rasbt.github.io/mlxtend/Code-of-Conduct/).

### Description

This PR updates Example 10 in the `plot_decision_regions` user guide to demonstrate how to provide custom class names (e.g., actual species names like 'Setosa') for the legend instead of generic placeholders. 

- Updated `plot_decision_regions.ipynb` with descriptive species labels.
- Updated the corresponding static image file in the `plot_decision_regions_files` directory to reflect these changes.

### Related issues or pull requests

Fixes #737

### Pull Request Checklist

Please fill out the following checklist if applicable. For more more information and help, please see the Contributor Documentation available at https://rasbt.github.io/mlxtend/contributing/.
-->

pullRequest.title: Fix #769 : Prevent double-wrapping of URLs in API generator
pullRequest.body:
### Code of Conduct

<!-- 
If this is your first Pull Request for the MLxtend repository, please review
the code of conduct, which is available at https://rasbt.github.io/mlxtend/Code-of-Conduct/. 
-->


### Description

This PR fixes the logic in make_api.py that was causing URLs to be "double-wrapped" in Markdown brackets (e.g., [[url](url)]([url](url))) on the documentation site. By adding a check in make_markdown_url, the generator now correctly ignores tokens that are already formatted as Markdown links, ensuring they render properly in the browser.

### Related issues or pull requests

Fixes #769

pullRequest.title: Fix incorrect R2 * -1 in "Example 4 – Regression and Adjusted R2" print() examples and modernize f-strings
pullRequest.body:
Hi maintainer 👋

This PR updates the documentation in Example 4:

✅ Removed incorrect R² multiplication by −1 in print() examples  
✅ Fixed reused/incorrect code from the previous example  
✅ Modernized old-style string formatting to f-strings for clarity  
✅ Optional: Casted num_examples to int for correctness & readability  

Fixes #1118

All changes were verified locally for syntax & output correctness.

Let me know if any further improvement is needed.

Thanks! 🚀

pullRequest.title: MAINT: remove deprecated multi_class="multinomial" from LogisticRegression usage
pullRequest.body:
This PR removes explicit `multi_class="multinomial"` arguments from
LogisticRegression usage in examples / notebooks / tests.

In sklearn ≥ 1.5 the `multi_class` parameter is deprecated and will be
removed in 1.7. The default behavior is now always `multinomial`, so
removing this avoids FutureWarning while keeping identical behavior.

✓ verified on sklearn ≥ 1.6
✓ warnings disappear
✓ outputs unchanged

Note: usages with `multi_class="ovr"` were intentionally left unchanged
to avoid altering behavior.

Closes #1126
